audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 2 Samuel / Shemuel Bet chapter 6, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for the thirty-thousand-person procession, ark title, new-cart transport, Uzzah's fatal contact, David's anger and fear, Obed-edom the Gittite, sacrifice, ecstatic dance, Michal's observation and protest, servant women, food distribution, exposure language, and childlessness.

verses.restored: David again gathered all chosen men in Israel, thirty thousand.
David arose and went with all people who were with him from Baale-judah to bring up from there ark of Divine, upon which Name is called, Name of YHWH of hosts, who sits above cherubim.
They mounted ark of Divine upon a new cart and carried it from house of Abinadab, which was on hill. Uzzah and Ahio, sons of Abinadab, were driving new cart.
They carried it from house of Abinadab, which was on hill, with ark of Divine, and Ahio was walking before ark.
David and all house of Israel were celebrating before YHWH with all kinds of cypress-wood instruments, with lyres, harps, tambourines, rattles/sistra, and cymbals.
They came to threshing floor of Nacon, and Uzzah reached toward ark of Divine and took hold of it, because cattle stumbled.
Anger of YHWH burned against Uzzah, and Divine struck him there for the error/irreverence, and he died there beside ark of Divine.
David became angry because YHWH had broken out in a breach against Uzzah. That place has been called Perez-uzzah until this day.
David was afraid of YHWH on that day and said, How can ark of YHWH come to me?
David was not willing to turn ark of YHWH toward himself into City of David. David turned it aside to house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
Ark of YHWH stayed in house of Obed-edom the Gittite three months, and YHWH blessed Obed-edom and all his house.

It was told to king David, saying, YHWH has blessed house of Obed-edom and all that belongs to him because of ark of Divine. David went and brought up ark of Divine from house of Obed-edom to City of David with joy.
When bearers of ark of YHWH had stepped six steps, he sacrificed an ox and a fattened animal.
David was whirling/dancing with all his strength before YHWH, and David was girded with a linen ephod.
David and all house of Israel brought up ark of YHWH with shouting and sound of shofar.
As ark of YHWH came into City of David, Michal daughter of Saul looked down through window and saw king David leaping and whirling before YHWH, and she despised him in her heart.
They brought ark of YHWH and set it in its place within tent David had pitched for it. David offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before YHWH.
David finished offering burnt offering and peace offerings, and he blessed people in name of YHWH of hosts.
He distributed to all people, to whole multitude of Israel, from man even to woman, to each one a loaf of bread, a portion of food/date cake, and a raisin cake. All people went, each to his house.

David returned to bless his house. Michal daughter of Saul came out to meet David and said, How glorious king of Israel was today, who uncovered himself today before eyes of maidservants of his servants, as one of vulgar men shamelessly uncovers himself.
David said to Michal, Before YHWH—who chose me above your father and above all his house, to appoint me leader over people of YHWH, over Israel—I celebrated before YHWH.
I will make myself still more contemptible than this and be low in my own eyes; yet with maidservants of whom you spoke, with them I will be honored.
Michal daughter of Saul had no child until day of her death.

translation_notes: Uzzah responds to a stumbling cart by stabilizing the ark and dies immediately. The chapter presents sacred violence; it must not be turned into victim-blaming, a workplace-safety slogan, or proof that accidental contact deserves death.
The transport involves a new cart, draft animals, multiple workers, musicians, and a mass procession. Religious spectacle does not remove organizational responsibility for preventable danger.
Obed-edom is identified as Gittite, likely linking a Philistine-associated identity to a household portrayed as blessed. Sacred presence crosses enemy categories.
The ark must not be treated as a magical object whose possession guarantees blessing, prosperity, political legitimacy, or protection.
Animal sacrifice and mass celebration carry material and animal costs that should not disappear beneath pageantry.

Michal watches from within the palace and objects to royal exposure before servant women. Her response may include class concern, sexual boundaries, public humiliation, dynastic trauma, or contempt; the narrator does not give a complete interior explanation.
David answers by invoking Divine choice over Michal's father and house, intensifying a marital conflict through political and theological dominance.
The servant women are discussed by both royal spouses but are given no speech. They are not props in an argument about David's authenticity.

Michal's childlessness is reported without an explicit causal statement. It may reflect infertility, separation, withheld intimacy, political confinement, or narrative closure; it must never be preached as punishment for criticizing worship.
The food distribution includes women explicitly and links celebration to material provision, though the exact food terms remain philologically uncertain.
safety_note: Maximum safeguards: Do not teach that Uzzah deserved death, that accidents reveal impure hearts, or that leaders may spiritualize unsafe systems. Do not vilify Michal or equate infertility with punishment. Protect bodily boundaries, worker safety, disabled and foreign-associated persons, servant women's dignity, and freedom from coerced religious spectacle.
